frameworks.name: functional connectivity (task-based fMRI)
frameworks.one_line: ROI-pair connectivity in language regions during convergent creative problem solving

core_question: Does acute stress alter functional connectivity among language-processing regions during a verbal creative problem-solving task, and is the effect modulated by gender or 5-HTTLPR genotype?
key_assumptions: convergent verbal problem solving (CRA) engages divergent-thinking processes relevant to creativity
MIST reliably induces psychosocial stress inside the scanner
S-allele of serotonin transporter indexes stress susceptibility
manipulations: stress vs no-stress session (modified MIST vs control)
compound remote associates trials interleaved with stressor blocks
key_findings: no main effect of stress on functional connectivity for individual language-ROI pairs
interpretations: stress does not produce uniform ROI-pair FC changes during this convergent verbal task
